title: Rootless networking setup not fully working with netavark
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

NB : can't test on 4.1.1 because no binaries available for Linux / can't compile it at the moment. 
Looking at the changelog however does not make me believe it is fixed right now

When running in non root, podman, with backend netavark seems to not lookup the network properly leading to 2 issue that prevent rootless container to have dns  with aardvark-dns.  As far as I can see dns for rootless is supported : https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/168

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```bash
# As non root user

#1st issue
podman system reset
podman network create rootless
podman --log-level=trace run -d --rm --name test0 --network=rootless jonlabelle/network-tools sleep infinity

# 2nd issue
# I Manually generated podman.json with 
# podman network inspect podman | jq '.[0] | .dns_enabled = true' --indent 5 > $HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/networks/podman.json
# to have dns_enabled for podman network. 
# podman network inspect podman show dns_enabled to true after that
podman --log-level=trace run -d --rm --name test2 --network=podman jonlabelle/network-tools sleep infinity
```

**Describe the results you received:**
1 > https://gist.github.com/sebastien-coavoux/91d4cffc30d8bde86a0ed20acaed6cd1
I would like to highlight in addition of the stack track at the end the lines
```
DEBU[0000] Successfully loaded 2 networks               
[...]
DEBU[0000] Successfully loaded 1 networks               
```
Which make me believe that network are found but somewhere in the validation info is not passed on

2 > https://gist.github.com/sebastien-coavoux/77cd3cf6f8df6ba112115eca799113f0
Highlighting netavark line :  
```
DEBU[0000] Successfully loaded network podman: &{podman 2f259bab93aaaaa2542ba43ef33eb990d0999ee1b9924b557b7be53c0b7a1bb9 bridge podman0 2022-06-29 21:12:46.176281014 +0000 UTC [{{{10.88.0.0 ffff0000}} 10.88.0.1 <nil>}] false false true map[] map[] map[driver:host-local]}
 
[...]

TRAC[0000] netavark command: printf '{"container_id":"1a2911922faf64a1d8b2bc4fb5455c531288f1a19ea0ac7e7019e02a04c97452","container_name":"test2","networks":{"podman":{"static_ips":["10.88.0.4"],"aliases":["1a2911922faf"],"interface_name":"eth0"}},"network_info":{"podman":{"name":"podman","id":"2f259bab93aaaaa2542ba43ef33eb990d0999ee1b9924b557b7be53c0b7a1bb9","driver":"bridge","network_interface":"podman0","created":"2022-06-29T21:19:45.707958376Z","subnets":[{"subnet":"10.88.0.0/16","gateway":"10.88.0.1"}],"ipv6_enabled":false,"internal":false, "dns_enabled":false, "ipam_options":{"driver":"host-local"}}}}' | /usr/libexec/podman/netavark setup /run/user/8000/netns/netns-ca2d29de-1421-395d-d4b6-ab091cb80980 
```
Here dns_enabled is false on the last line but true on the first (third boolen is true)

**Describe the results you expected:**
1 > The pod running and aardvark-dns started as low user
2 > aardvark-dns started as low user

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Logic setup seems to reside in common. Some snippet in  believe the code end up reaching : 

https://github.com/containers/common/blob/0b482d01472ecf915fec93aed49c385b68c3a740/libnetwork/netavark/run.go#L32 (validation)
https://github.com/containers/common/blob/0b482d01472ecf915fec93aed49c385b68c3a740/libnetwork/netavark/run.go#L65 (log dumping command)
https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/libnetwork/netavark/network.go#L272 (get network)

**Output of `podman version`:**

No - See Note at the very beginning

comments.body:
Hi @sebastien-coavoux , Thanks for creating the issue.

For the 1st part i,e panic in podman i think issue is with older libnetwork could you please try using newer version. @Luap99 Could confirm this better.

~~For the 2nd part I don't think `dns` in supported on default network i.e network : `podman` see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/basic_networking.md#default-network~~

Edit: default network can support dns if explicitly configured.

There is no panic. This is a simple stack trace from the error which is the normal behaviour on log level trace.

@flouthoc dns can/should work on any network when the setting is turned on


Both issues are likely the same, the problem is that podman cannot see the networks in the rootless netns which is weird.
Please run `podman unshare --rootless-netns` and check if the `$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/networks/` is there?

There is a better workaround, `mkdir /var/lib/cni`. 
The rootless network namespace steup is very complicated. CNI was not designed to work in this configuration, so I had to create workarounds by bind mounting directories so that rootless could write to them.

Now that we have netavark we could lift some of the workarounds.

Ok, I can confirm that simply creating `/var/lib/cni` as root make the command above work ! 

Feel free to close , I don't know if I missed that somewhere in the doc . 

Thanks !
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
